---
title: "Making_Maps"
editor: visual
---

> ## Why make a map?
>
> blah blah **blah.** blah 
Maps help place environmental and ecological data into a spatial context. They allow researchers to visualise: 
- **where** observations were made
- identify **geographic patterns**
- **communicate** results more effectively. 
Including a map can greatly improve the interpretation of ecological processes by showing how species, habitats, and environmental conditions are distributed acrossyour study area.


#### How to make an effective and visually appealing map: 




Setting up print layout <https://youtu.be/ojRrIZ0M8o4?si=UH-Or13CySDlbvsk>

Drawing beautiful maps programmatically with R, sf and ggplot2 — Part 1: Basics
